Primary capability / role: Primary API + webhook receiver + UI backend + event handlers; scale-to-zero ideal for bursty operations
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Request-based free tier includes 2M requests, 180k vCPU-seconds, 360k GiB-seconds per month (billing-account scoped), then billed per vCPU-second / GiB-second and per million requests.

Primary capability / role: Lightweight glue code: webhook endpoints, Pub/Sub handlers, scheduled jobs
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Use for “small, single-purpose” handlers; costs align closely with Cloud Run request-based pricing patterns.

Primary capability / role: Orchestrate multi-step processes (order -> calendar -> tasks -> email -> invoice); durable retries & error handling
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Always-free: 5,000 internal steps + 2,000 external HTTP calls monthly; then $0.01/1k internal steps, $0.025/1k external steps.

Primary capability / role: Event bus for decoupling: order.created, invoice.sent, inventory.low
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Always-free: first 10 GiB throughput per month; then throughput billed (e.g., $40/TiB in standard regions).

Primary capability / role: Cron-like triggers: daily inventory checks, weekly labor report, nightly sync
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Billed per job definition (not per execution): $0.10/job/31 days; there is a small free allowance (commonly cited as first 3 jobs/month).

Primary capability / role: Reliable retries for side effects: send emails/SMS, generate PDFs, write to external systems
Constraints / dependencies / cost: First 1M operations/month free, then $0.40 per million operations (chunked).

Primary capability / role: Alternative to Cloud Run for some web apps; can host internal dashboards
Constraints / dependencies / cost: App Engine Standard has Free Tier instance-hour allowances (e.g., daily free quotas for certain instance classes) but can be less flexible than Cloud Run for modern container workflows.

Primary capability / role: “Managed VM” approach: run a monolith, cron jobs, legacy integrations
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Free tier includes one e2-micro VM plus limited disk/egress in specific US regions; beyond that, VM costs + disk + networking apply.

Primary capability / role: Kubernetes-based microservices, advanced networking/policies, multi-service ops
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Has a cluster management fee of $0.10/cluster-hour (≈$72-$73/month baseline) plus node compute/storage.

Primary capability / role: Store contracts, BEOs, menus, invoices, photos, logs exports
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Charged by storage class + operations + egress; supports bucket-level location choice and features like object versioning / lifecycle policies for cost control.

Primary capability / role: Operational system-of-record for events/orders/menus/tasks; flexible for rapid iteration
Constraints / dependencies / cost: One free database; free tier includes 1 GiB storage, 50k reads/day, 20k writes/day, 10 GiB outbound/month. PITR/backups are billable features.

Primary capability / role: Relational back office (invoices, line items, inventory ledger) when strict SQL constraints needed
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Shared-core pricing starts around $0.0105/hour for db-f1-micro (plus storage, backups, networking); PITR and automated backups available.

Primary capability / role: Cache hot data (menus, route lookups), rate-limit tokens, session store
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Often expensive for small budgets (example pricing shows large monthly costs even at modest sizes). Avoid unless you have clear performance need.

Primary capability / role: Reporting: lead sources, conversion, food cost, labor %, vendor spend, delivery SLA; billing analytics
Constraints / dependencies / cost: BigQuery has a free tier and free operations; you can also export Cloud Billing data into BigQuery for cost governance.

Primary capability / role: Roles, service accounts, least-privilege service-to-service auth
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Core to controlling who can invoke services / access data; service account best practices are explicitly documented.

Primary capability / role: Store API keys (CRM, vendors, SMS), DB creds, signing keys
Constraints / dependencies / cost: 6 secret versions free; access operations charged after free tier; Cloud Run can be configured to access secrets via IAM role bindings.

Primary capability / role: Private networking, egress control, private service access (e.g., private DB)
Constraints / dependencies / cost: VPC itself is foundational; costs typically appear via egress, NAT, load balancing, etc. Networking pricing applies during and after free trial (charged against credits in trial).

Primary capability / role: Monitoring, alerting, uptime checks, dashboards
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Some Monitoring features have free allotments (e.g., 1M uptime check executions/month), then metered charges.

Primary capability / role: Operational audit trail; troubleshooting workflows and order flows
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Log ingestion has a free tier commonly referenced as first 50 GiB/month, then ~$0.50/GiB ingestion; retention beyond 30 days billed at $0.01/GiB-month. Manage via exclusions/retention.

Topic: Billing governance - Cloud Billing API + Budgets
Primary capability / role: Automate guardrails and reporting; programmatic budget notifications to Pub/Sub
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Budgets can trigger Pub/Sub notifications; Billing export to BigQuery supports cost analytics.

Primary capability / role: Delivery routing, geocoding venues, address validation, ETA estimates
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Since March 1, 2025: pricing uses free monthly calls per SKU (Essentials commonly 10k free calls/SKU). Route compute and geocoding have explicit caps and per-1,000-event pricing.

Primary capability / role: Receipt/photo scanning, label/OCR, basic image understanding
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Free tier: 1,000 units/month; quickstarts explicitly note the free allowance.

Primary capability / role: Voice-based order capture; transcribe voicemails
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Free tier: 60 minutes/month.

Primary capability / role: Parse invoices, contracts, banquet event orders (BEOs), W-9s; structured extraction
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Paid per processing; positioned as “try free” via credits for new customers; use sparingly unless you have volume economics.

Primary capability / role: Temperature logger ingestion + alerts; cold chain compliance
Constraints / dependencies / cost: IoT Core was retired Aug 16, 2023; implement IoT ingestion using generic endpoints (Cloud Run + Pub/Sub) or partner platforms.

Topic: CI/CD (recommended) - Cloud Build + Artifact Registry + Cloud Deploy
Primary capability / role: Build/deploy Cloud Run services reliably
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Cloud Build has free build-minutes allowances (documented in Free Tier tables); Artifact Registry has a small free storage tier; Cloud Deploy pricing includes a free first active multi-target pipeline and $5/month for additional multi-target pipelines.

Topic: Order intake (web, email, form, CRM lead)
Primary capability / role: Start with one Cloud Run endpoint /orders that validates, writes an Order doc, and publishes order.created. A Workflows execution then creates/updates: a calendar event, prep plan, and customer confirmation email.
Outcome / level: Triggers: Web form submit; inbound email; CRM webhook
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Cloud Run (HTTP) + Firestore + Pub/Sub + Workflows

Primary capability / role: Implement menu items as Firestore documents. For adoption, add a “Sheets sync” job (read/write via Sheets API) so the team can keep using familiar sheets during transition.
Outcome / level: Triggers: Internal admin edits; seasonal updates
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Firestore + Cloud Run admin UI; optionally Sheets as UI surface

Primary capability / role: Track inventory deltas per event. Nightly job computes “projected stock” and flags low items. Export snapshots to BigQuery later for analysis (food cost trends).
Outcome / level: Triggers: After event closeout; scheduled nightly reconciliation
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Firestore + Cloud Scheduler + Cloud Run job + BigQuery (optional)

Primary capability / role: Generate a purchase list (by vendor/category) and push to a shared Sheet; email vendors automatically with a draft PO (PDF stored in Cloud Storage). Use Cloud Tasks for retries.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Pub/Sub + Workflows + Cloud Tasks + Gmail/Sheets APIs

Primary capability / role: Implement a deterministic scaling service: input (recipe_id, servings, dietary constraints) -> output scaled ingredient quantities. Store results per event as an immutable “prep plan” version.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Cloud Run job/function + Firestore

Primary capability / role: Workflow maintains a canonical event schedule in Firestore, then writes “staffing needs” rows to Sheets and creates Calendar blocks for shifts. The extraction emphasizes calendar-driven planning and labor allocation pressure.
Outcome / level: Triggers: New event confirmed; weekly labor planning
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Workflows + Calendar API + Sheets API

Primary capability / role: MVI: geocode/validate addresses, compute a route, store ETAs; share route link to driver. Keep within free SKU caps first (Essentials often 10k free calls).
Outcome / level: Triggers: Delivery window set; address confirmed
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Cloud Run + Routes API + (optional) Route Optimization API

Primary capability / role: Email is simplest and low-cost to start (Gmail API). Use Cloud Tasks for retry/backoff and idempotency keys to avoid duplicate sends. SMS can be added later via an external provider.
Outcome / level: Triggers: Order confirmation; reminders; delivery updates
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Cloud Tasks + Gmail API; optional SMS via entity["company","Twilio","sms platform"]

Primary capability / role: MVI: Generate invoice PDFs, store in Cloud Storage, email via Gmail API, update payment status in Firestore. If you need relational accounting rigor, introduce Cloud SQL later; pricing starts at small shared-core rates but it’s easy to overspend if left running 24/7.
Outcome / level: Triggers: Event completion; deposit due; balance due
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Cloud Run + Firestore/Cloud SQL + Cloud Storage + Gmail API

Primary capability / role: Since IoT Core is retired, ingest device telemetry via HTTPS/MQTT endpoints you operate (Cloud Run) then store in Firestore and alert on thresholds. Retain logs per policy; add exports for audits.
Outcome / level: Triggers: Periodic sensor reads; manual checklists
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Cloud Run ingestion + Pub/Sub + Firestore; IoT Core replacement approach

Primary capability / role: Record feedback responses; compute NPS-like metrics; push summary to analytics. Automate “request review” emails 24-72 hours after event completion.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Cloud Run + Firestore + BigQuery + Gmail API

Primary capability / role: Store lead source and stage transitions as events for later attribution analysis. Use BigQuery for funnel reporting once volumes grow.
Outcome / level: Triggers: New lead; deal stage changes
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Cloud Run webhook + Pub/Sub + BigQuery

Primary capability / role: Maintain venue/contact entities with assigned “owner” rep; scheduled reminders create tasks and emails. Optionally enrich addresses via Places/Geocoding.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Firestore + Scheduler + Workflows + Maps Places (optional)

Primary capability / role: vCPU-seconds, GiB-seconds, and requests beyond free tier
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Often $0 if inside free tier (2M req + 180k vCPU-sec + 360k GiB-sec). Above that, request-based billing applies.

Primary capability / role: reads/writes/storage beyond free allowance; paid features like PITR/backups
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Often $0 at modest scale if you stay under free daily quotas; beware that PITR/backups are billable features.

Primary capability / role: throughput beyond 10 GiB/month
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Often $0; spikes are usually still small unless streaming large payloads.

Primary capability / role: steps beyond free
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Typically pennies to a few dollars; free: 5,000 internal + 2,000 external monthly.

Primary capability / role: operations beyond 1M/month
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Usually $0 in early phases; first 1M operations free.

Primary capability / role: job definitions beyond small free allowance
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Low single digits per month unless you create many schedules ($0.10/job/31 days).

Primary capability / role: per-1,000 event pricing beyond free SKU caps
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Often $0 initially because Essentials commonly include 10k free calls/SKU; route compute is $5 per 1,000 after free cap in Essentials.

Primary capability / role: ingestion beyond free amounts + retention beyond 30 days
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Can become material; first ~50 GiB/month often free, then about $0.50/GiB; retention >30 days $0.01/GiB-month.

Primary capability / role: Cloud Run + Firestore + Workflows + Pub/Sub + Tasks/Scheduler
Outcome / level: High; add services and load without re-platforming
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Pros: Lowest ops burden; scale-to-zero; aligns with bursty catering workflows; cost largely proportional to usage | Cons: Requires careful event/idempotency design; local dev patterns differ from VM-style “always on” | Est. monthly cost: $0-$75 typical if staying in free tiers and controlling logs; spikes if Maps/AI heavy

Primary capability / role: Kubernetes (GKE) + multiple services; internal service mesh/network controls
Outcome / level: Very high, but only worth it when complexity/traffic justify
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Pros: Strong for complex multi-service platforms; rich policy controls; standard microservice tooling | Cons: Baseline cluster fee plus nodes; higher SRE burden and tuning; usually poor fit for $300-credit MVP | Est. monthly cost: $150-$500+ depending on nodes; baseline includes $0.10/cluster-hour (~$72-$73/month) plus compute/storage

Primary capability / role: Compute Engine VM(s) running monolith + cron; optional Cloud SQL
Outcome / level: Medium; scales but at growing ops complexity
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Pros: Familiar ops model; can be cheap if using free e2-micro tier and very small workload | Cons: Patch/ops burden; scaling is manual; uptime costs when left running; risk of “accidental always-on” spend | Est. monthly cost: $0-$200 depending on VM sizing and whether you run Cloud SQL 24/7; free tier exists for e2-micro in certain regions

Primary capability / role: Cursor agents accelerate schema design, CRUD endpoints, UI scaffolds, tests.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Production DB + backend hosting (e.g., Postgres + app server).

Primary capability / role: Agent-assisted data modeling (constraints), UI state, validation rules.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Rule engine in app; careful pricing calculation + idempotent checkout.

Primary capability / role: Cursor helps implement tagging system, search queries, UI filters, admin tooling.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Data governance and operational rigor (accuracy); optional compliance workflows.

Primary capability / role: Cursor generates proposal workflow code + email templates; can build admin review UI quickly.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Email service + e-sign provider if needed; CRM integration (Close).

Primary capability / role: Cursor helps implement time-slot availability, cutoffs, and scheduling logic; tests for edge cases.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Calendar system + inventory constraints; background jobs for reminders.

Primary capability / role: Cursor speeds integration code + webhook handler scaffolding; encourages idempotency patterns.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Payments platform + webhooks; signature verification; PCI scope management.

Primary capability / role: Cursor generates templating + notification routing logic; test harnesses.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Email/SMS provider + webhook callbacks/status tracking.

Primary capability / role: Cursor helps implement auth flows, RBAC, account pages.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Auth provider or custom auth; security hardening.

Primary capability / role: Rapid admin UI generation + validation rules + migration scripts.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: DB migrations + role permissions.

Primary capability / role: Cursor helps implement CRM-synced lead mirror, dashboards, reporting.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Close API integration + webhooks; rate limit handling.

Topic: Lead source attribution + referral quality tracking
Primary capability / role: Cursor helps model attribution across sources; build analytics queries.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Enforced data entry + integration touches in intake forms/CRM.

Primary capability / role: Cursor helps implement event-sourcing or status history tables.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Consistent status updates (CRM ↔ internal); reconciliation jobs.

Primary capability / role: Cursor accelerates calendar integration + templated tasting note capture.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Calendar provider push notifications; channel renewals.

Primary capability / role: Cursor helps implement commission rules + payout reports.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Payroll/accounting integration if automated; audits.

Topic: Weekly close visibility + cash forecasting tied to pipeline
Primary capability / role: Cursor helps implement forecast models, snapshots, scheduled reporting jobs.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Data warehouse/reporting or scheduled compute; finance data sources.

Primary capability / role: Cursor helps implement time tracking ingestion + reporting views.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Timekeeping input method (mobile/timeclock) or manual entry; exports.

Primary capability / role: Cursor speeds building scheduling UI + constraints and optimization heuristics.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Potential optimizer/job solver; integrations with calendar and payroll.

Topic: Calendar as “operational truth” (single source of truth)
Primary capability / role: Cursor can accelerate building bidirectional sync + conflict resolution logic.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Calendar provider quotas/limits + watch renewal + dedupe.

Topic: Jobs/events with rich operational detail fields
Primary capability / role: Cursor benefits: schema definition + admin forms + validation + attachments.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: File storage for BEOs/attachments; access controls.

Topic: Visual readability scheduling board (color coding, at-a-glance complexity)
Primary capability / role: Cursor accelerates UI implementation (calendar board/time grid) + UX iteration.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Frontend framework + state mgmt; performance tuning for large schedules.

Topic: Job complexity scoring (e.g., grill on-site, staff count, timeline)
Primary capability / role: Cursor helps formalize scoring model and compute derived fields.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Requires operations-defined rubric + continuous iteration.

Primary capability / role: Cursor can build receipt submission pipeline and reimbursement ledger.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Optional accounting integration; document storage.

Primary capability / role: Cursor speeds scheduled reports + dashboards.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Payments, invoices, payroll, AP sources; reconciliation.

Topic: Shared shopping / prep list tied to weekly schedule
Primary capability / role: Cursor helps build “event -> ingredients -> aggregated purchase list” logic.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Inventory + recipe yields; mobile-friendly checklist UX.

Primary capability / role: Cursor speeds building role dashboards + task assignment + permissions.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Task system integration (ClickUp) or native tasks.

Primary capability / role: Cursor helps implement CRM-style entities, reminders, and reporting.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: CRM sync and/or dedicated partner module.

Primary capability / role: Same as venues; Cursor helps implement entity + touchpoints + reminders.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: CRM integration; comms logging.

Primary capability / role: Cursor helps implement search/rank logic + geo filters.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Geocoding/maps provider; data curation.

Topic: Google Sheets import/export as operating surface
Primary capability / role: Cursor can generate robust ETL sync jobs and admin “export” tools quickly.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Sheets API quotas + batching + backoff.

Topic: Close CRM as sales authority (bi-directional sync)
Primary capability / role: Cursor accelerates API client + webhook ingestion, signature verification.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Close webhooks + signatures + rate limits.

Primary capability / role: Cursor can implement webhook posting + structured messages quickly.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Slack webhooks/app config; message formatting governance.

Primary capability / role: Cursor helps implement task mirroring + webhook-driven updates.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: ClickUp webhooks and API; handling payload/trigger semantics.

Primary capability / role: Cursor helps add instrumentation consistently + generate dashboards-as-code.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Observability stack + collectors/exporters.

Primary capability / role: Cursor helps implement append-only logs + admin views + alerting.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Storage, retention policies, access controls.

Primary capability / role: Cursor can implement export jobs, deletion flows, and proofs-of-deletion.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Must meet right to erasure + portability requirements.

Primary capability / role: Cursor can scaffold idempotent webhook handlers and event dedupe.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Vendor webhook retries; store processed event IDs.

Primary capability / role: Use Projects + custom GPTs to document SOPs, roles, and operational artifacts; generate process maps and playbooks.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: “Documentation” is feasible; “automation” still needs triggers + data plumbing. | Inputs: Existing SOP docs, sample events, existing spreadsheet templates.
Action / implementation note: “Process mining prompt”: ask for workflow, produce swimlanes + checklists + RACI.

Primary capability / role: Draft responses, proposals, qualification scripts; analyze lead notes; use email/calendar apps for context.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: No reliable end-to-end “new lead -> CRM update -> follow-up sequence” without external automation (and/or CRM app availability). | Inputs: CRM export CSV; email threads; proposal templates in Drive.
Action / implementation note: “Triage -> schema -> draft”: extract lead fields to JSON then generate email/proposal.

Topic: Lead source awareness matters a lot
Primary capability / role: Data analysis on lead source fields for conversion, close time, and revenue attribution.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Requires consistently captured data; may require ETL from CRM into a sheet. | Inputs: CSV export from CRM; standardized “source” taxonomy.
Action / implementation note: “Analytics prompt”: compute funnel by source; return charts + narrative + actions.

Primary capability / role: GPT Plus can advise and generate updates, but cannot natively act in Close unless there is an app or you use GPT Actions (which requires an external API endpoint).
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Missing native Close app + no Plus-as-backend. Practical automation requires Zapier/API glue. | Inputs: Close API / Zapier sync into Sheets; or migrate to a CRM with a ChatGPT app.
Action / implementation note: “CRM delta prompt”: produce “what to update” payloads for manual entry or downstream automation.

Primary capability / role: GPT can analyze pipeline tracker sheets and generate weekly forecast narratives and scenarios.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Scheduled automation is limited (Tasks can’t use file uploads or GPTs). | Inputs: Pipeline sheet; assumptions for probability and expected close date.
Action / implementation note: “Forecast with assumptions”: compute base/upside/downside and confidence notes.

Primary capability / role: Upload timesheets/payroll exports; compute labor cost per event, per role, per worker; generate a weekly labor report.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Data capture quality; no guaranteed writeback into payroll/HR. | Inputs: Timesheet export; event roster; pay rates table.
Action / implementation note: “Labor report prompt”: enforce schema; output summary + anomalies list.

Topic: Scheduling is a major operational function
Primary capability / role: Use calendar apps to summarize upcoming events and detect conflicts; generate staffing plans and run-of-show. (Calendar apps may be read-only depending on provider/app.)
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Creating/modifying calendar events is not reliably supported across plans/providers; agent mode is capped. | Inputs: Calendar events; staffing availability; travel-time heuristics.
Action / implementation note: “Schedule digest prompt”: produce daily/weekly operational brief + staffing needs.

Topic: Calendar is a core source of operational truth
Primary capability / role: Projects can reference Drive/Slack links; apps can search calendar/email for context.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: “Truth source” still needs normalization and structured extraction. | Inputs: Calendar + linked BEO/Docs; standardized naming conventions.
Action / implementation note: “Calendar -> ops schema”: extract event objects into normalized JSON rows.

Primary capability / role: GPT excels at converting complex event text (BEOs, emails) into structured checklists, timelines, staffing plans.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: If BEO is PDF/image, extraction quality varies; tasks can’t use file uploads; human review required. | Inputs: BEO PDF; venue docs; equipment inventory list.
Action / implementation note: “Extract -> validate -> generate”: 3-pass approach with explicit validation rules.

Primary capability / role: GPT can propose dashboard layouts and generate “one-page ops brief” formats; can generate tables/charts from sheets.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Cannot guarantee UI rendering in Google Sheets UI; you still implement formatting. | Inputs: Ops sheet; color conventions; column schema.
Action / implementation note: “Dashboard spec prompt”: output a layout spec + formulas + conditional formatting rules.

Topic: Some jobs are more complex than others
Primary capability / role: Build a complexity scoring rubric; use GPT to classify events and suggest staffing/equipment.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Needs historical outcomes to calibrate; risk of hallucinated constraints without data. | Inputs: Past events dataset (event attributes + staff hours + issues).
Action / implementation note: “Scoring rubric prompt”: produce score + reason codes + resource plan.

Primary capability / role: GPT can extract receipt fields (amount, category, event) from uploads and produce reimbursement entries; create summaries.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Must avoid cardholder data; cannot automate payments; needs accounting system integration for full flow. | Inputs: Receipts; chart of accounts; reimbursement policy doc.
Action / implementation note: “Receipt extraction”: output a strict CSV row set + confidence markers.

Topic: Financial visibility is weekly and practical
Primary capability / role: GPT can synthesize cash outlook reports from spreadsheet data and narrative assumptions with scenario analysis.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Requires good data hygiene; true automation needs scheduled ingestion from bank/processor (not Plus). | Inputs: Weekly cash sheet; expected withdrawals/payroll; vendor bills.
Action / implementation note: “Weekly CFO brief”: table + risks + recommended moves next 7/14 days.

Primary capability / role: GPT can generate scaled shopping lists, prep sequencing, and batch plans from menus and headcount; can output into sheets/docs if the Drive actions are enabled for your plan.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Ingredient database + recipes must exist; write actions may depend on config; human verification for food safety/allergens. | Inputs: Recipe library; inventory on hand; suppliers; allergen flags.
Action / implementation note: “Menu -> ingredient BOM”: produce per-item BOM + consolidated purchase order.

Primary capability / role: GPT can generate role-based checklists, shift briefs, and SOP snippets; can summarize ClickUp if connected.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: ClickUp “sync” documentation indicates read scope today; creating/updating tasks may not be available without write actions or external automation. | Inputs: Task templates; roles; event schema.
Action / implementation note: “Checklist compiler”: output per role, per day, with dependencies and time estimates.

Primary capability / role: GPT can draft outreach sequences, meeting agendas, and monthly check-in scripts; summarize venue notes if stored in docs.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Relationship system needs CRM writeback + reminders; tasks limited to 10 active tasks, so use batch cadence tasks not per-venue tasks. | Inputs: Venue database; contact list; last-touch dates; deal history.
Action / implementation note: “Relationship cadence”: generate next-touch plan + email drafts + logging payload.

Primary capability / role: Same as venues: communication templates and follow-ups.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Automated sequence execution requires CRM/email automation tooling. | Inputs: Planner list; tone guidelines; prior messages.
Action / implementation note: “Planner follow-up”: draft + subject lines + versions by tone.

Primary capability / role: GPT can generate owner dashboards and “overdue touchpoints”; produce accountability reports.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Requires correct assignment data + reminders; Plus has limited governance for multi-user workflows. | Inputs: Assignment sheet; weekly cadence; success metrics.
Action / implementation note: “Owner report”: produce per-rep tasks next 7 days + context snippets.

Primary capability / role: GPT can recommend vendors based on an internal vendor list + event constraints; can supplement with web research.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Needs curated vendor data; web results need verification; no guaranteed geo or pricing accuracy. | Inputs: Vendor list; service areas; pricing; SLAs; venue restrictions.
Action / implementation note: “Constraint satisfaction prompt”: explicit constraints + ranked recommendation table.

Topic: Tastings are a real sales mechanism
Primary capability / role: GPT can generate tasting agendas, feedback forms, and conversion follow-up sequences; summarize tasting notes (including from record mode).
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Scheduling/booking is not fully automated; capture of evaluations must be structured. | Inputs: Tasting form; rubric; sample menus; pricing sheet.
Action / implementation note: “Tasting debrief”: extract objections + next steps + tailored proposal delta.

Primary capability / role: GPT can compute commissions from a sheet and produce payout reports and exception flags.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Needs consistent deal attribution; integration to payroll/accounting not included. | Inputs: Deal close export; commission rules; adjustments log.
Action / implementation note: “Commission engine prompt”: compute + audit trail + edge case list.

Primary capability / role: Data analysis + sheet-friendly outputs (CSV, formulas, normalized tables) are a core strength.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Human still validates outputs; writeback depends on app actions configuration. | Inputs: Clean, normalized sheets; consistent headers.
Action / implementation note: “Sheet-first prompt”: output CSV/TSV + formulas + validation rules.

Primary capability / role: Google Drive app/actions can unify Docs/Sheets/Slides actions; connected Google data has specific privacy rules.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Workspace admin settings can affect actions; project-link sources may not support sync in-project. | Inputs: Drive folders per event; templates; shared permissions.
Action / implementation note: “Doc assembly prompt”: create proposal/BEO addendum from templates + variables.

Primary capability / role: GPT can reconcile “where truth lives,” propose consolidation, generate migration plans, and produce bridges (exports/import templates).
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Technical integration across tools is outside Plus-only; requires API/no-code automation layer. | Inputs: Inventory of systems + fields; sample exports from each.
Action / implementation note: “System mapping prompt”: produce canonical schema + mapping tables + migration steps.

Primary capability / role: GPT can generate role-specific materials, but Plus is a personal plan-no enterprise workspace controls, RBAC, or “no training by default” posture like Business.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Secure multi-user deployment is constrained; account sharing prohibited. | Inputs: If multi-user: individual accounts + shared templates; or move to Business plan.
Action / implementation note: “Role pack prompt”: produce a per-role playbook + standard prompts + QA checklist.

Topic: Structure should scale through people and systems
Primary capability / role: GPT can produce scalable SOPs, training materials, and structured templates.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Systems scaling needs integrations, permissions, and auditability beyond Plus. | Inputs: SOP docs; training rubrics; standard outputs.
Action / implementation note: “Training module prompt”: create onboarding lessons + scenarios + rubrics.

Primary capability / role: GPT can produce weekly exec briefs and KPI dashboards from sheets.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: KPI extraction depends on data consistency; deep research is not for ops KPI automation. | Inputs: KPI sheet; thresholds; definitions.
Action / implementation note: “Ops dashboard prompt”: compute KPIs + anomalies + actions.

Primary capability / role: Scheduled Tasks can generate recurring briefs and reminders; limited to 10 active tasks; runs offline and notifies you.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Tasks can’t use file uploads or GPTs; keep tasks lightweight and rely on stable text instructions. | Inputs: Fixed weekly prompt text; links to sheets/docs.
Action / implementation note: “Weekly cadence task”: stable prompt + explicit output format + escalation rules.

Primary capability / role: Same as weekly rhythms; plus can generate cycle checklists and seasonal playbooks.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: True operational orchestration requires event-driven automation outside Plus. | Inputs: Cycle definitions; triggers; owners.
Action / implementation note: “Cycle orchestrator prompt”: generate checklists + dependencies + owner routing.

Primary capability / role: GPT excels at converting messy notes into structured plans, checklists, and tables.
Constraints / dependencies / cost: Needs disciplined inputs; without structured data you’ll get drift. | Inputs: Standard intake forms; canonical schema.
Action / implementation note: “Normalization-first”: force JSON schema, then generate artifacts.

Primary capability / role: GPT Plus can reduce time spent drafting/formatting/reporting and improve consistency; but it won’t replace system integration.
